Joinder To Defendant Cynthia Hacker'S Motion To Change Venue And For Request For Attorney Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Sep-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Scott Fraser for Plaintiff.
John L. Flegel (650/324-9300; jlf@jsmf.com) of Jorgenson, Siegel, McClure & Flegel, LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT HARRY LITKE's Joinder To Defendant Cynthia Hacker'S Motion To Change Venue And For Request For Attorney Fees And Costs:

Joinder is granted, and the motion to change venue is denied for the reasons stated in the tentative ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Defendant Cynthia Hackers Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue
Off Calendar Sep-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Scott Fraser for Plaintiff.
John L. Flegel (650/324-9300; jlf@jsmf.com) of Jorgenson, Siegel, McClure & Flegel, LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT CYNTHIA HACKER's Motion To Change Venue:

Defendant Cynthia Hacker's motion to change venue is denied. Plaintiff's petition seeks, among other things, an order of interment pursuant to Health & Safety Code section 7100 directing the construction of the Litke Family Mausoleum and the interment of Decedent Edward Litke's remains therein. Such a petition may be filed "in the superior court in the county in which the decedent resided at the time of his or her death, or in which the remains are located . . . ." (Health & Safety Code sec. 7105(c).) Here, decedent was a resident of San Francisco at the time of his death. (Pet. par. 1.) Thus, venue in this Court is proper. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 395(a) [general rule that action must be commenced in the county where one or more defendants reside does not apply if "otherwise provided by law"].) Defendant's reliance on Health & Safety Code section 9069.10 is misplaced. Health & Safety Code chapter 5.5, sections 9069.10-9069.40, in which that statute is found, "does not apply to, or prohibit, the removal of remains from one plot to another in the same cemetery." (Health & Safety Code sec. 9069.15.) The parties agree that decedent's remains are currently interred in the Home of Peace Cemetery in Colma, and Plaintiff is seeking to remove those remains to another mausoleum to be constructed in the same cemetery.

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
